name: Gartxot's Trail
name_original: Ruta de Gartxot – circular desde Izalzu/Itzaltzu
description: Hike the Ruta de Gartxot, a moderate 7.2-mile (11.5 km) circular trail through the Irati Forest, featuring waterfalls, Pyrenees views, and t
region_intro: Hiking around Izalzu/Itzaltzu offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Navarre region, characterized by extensive beech and fir forests, significant mountain passes, and elevated peaks. The area features varied terrain, from serene forest trails in the Irati Forest to challenging ascents leading to panoramic vistas. This region provides a range of elevations, including the highest point in the Basque Country, Pic d'Orhy, at 2,017 meters. Best hiking trails around Izalzu/Itzaltzu The most popular hiking route is Gartxot's Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local terrain, often passing through forested areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pic d'Orhy – Irati-Abodi Mountain Center loop from Ollokia, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading to views from Pic d'Orhy. Local hikers also love the Ochagavía loop from Ochagavía / Otsagabia, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through varied local scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Izalzu/Itzaltzu is defined by ancient beech and fir forests, mountain passes, and panoramic vistas from high peaks. The network offers options for easy walks as well as more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Izalzu/Itzaltzu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 290 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Izalzu/Itzaltzu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Izalzu/Itzaltzu?
A: Izalzu/Itzaltzu offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from serene forest paths within the extensive Irati Forest to challenging mountain ascents leading to panoramic views. You'll find routes suitable for various skill levels, including easy walks and more demanding climbs to high peaks.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ochagavía loop from Ochagavía / Otsagabia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile (5.2 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 35 minutes to complete, offering varied local scenery.

Q: What are some of the more challenging hikes in the area?
A: For experienced hikers, the region offers challenging routes with significant elevation gain. A notable difficult trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy – Irati-Abodi Mountain Center loop from Ollokia&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) path leading to the summit of Pic d'Orhy. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15007497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orhitxipia (1,925 m) – Pic d'Orhy loop from Pikatua&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What notable natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and significant landmarks. You can explore the vast Irati Forest, one of Europe's best-preserved beech and fir forests. Hikers can also reach &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Basque Country, offering panoramic views. Other notable features include mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrau Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/941372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laza Pass (1,129 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the Izalzu/Itzaltzu area are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40320500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaztanbidea (1,460 m) – Abodi (1,531 m) loop from Pikatua&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9 km circular hike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ochagavía loop from Ochagavía / Otsagabia&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: Is the Irati Forest accessible for hikers, and what can I expect there?
A: The Irati Forest is highly accessible and a major highlight for hikers. It's one of Europe's largest and best-preserved beech and fir forests, offering countless trails for all skill levels. Within the forest, you'll find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Houses&lt;/a&gt;, which provide amenities like a bar, restaurant, and bike shop, making it a charming base for exploration.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Izalzu/Itzaltzu?
A: The region offers distinct experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn are particularly popular due to pleasant temperatures and vibrant foliage, especially in the beech and fir forests. Summer is also suitable, though some higher altitude trails might be more exposed. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but requires appropriate gear and checking local conditions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of numerous easy trails and the extensive, relatively flat areas within the Irati Forest suggest many suitable options for families. Look for shorter, less strenuous loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15006804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ochagavía loop from Ochagavía / Otsagabia&lt;/a&gt; for a pleasant outing with children.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Izalzu/Itzaltzu?
A: The routes in Izalzu/Itzaltzu are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.6 stars from over 290 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, the well-preserved forests, and the variety of trails catering to different abilities.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Izalzu/Itzaltzu?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not listed, Izalzu/Itzaltzu and nearby villages like Ochagavía typically offer parking facilities for visitors. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for details on trailhead access and parking availability.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Izalzu/Itzaltzu region?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for hiking on the established trails in the Izalzu/Itzaltzu region, including the Irati Forest. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land, and to check for any temporary restrictions before your hike.
tips: From this panoramic point on the Ruta del Gartxot there are beautiful views towards the Pyrenees and with good visibility you can see the Moncayo.
This beautiful waterfall is located on the path of the Ruta del Gartxot surrounded by a wonderful and picturesque forest of beech and oak trees.

This cave owes its name to a medieval legend that tells that the bard Gartxot took refuge there after murdering his son in order to prevent him from being snatched by the Orreaga / Roncesvalles monks. It is located in a nice forest setting.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Ruta de Gartxot?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Izalzu/Itzaltzu. You can typically find parking available within the village itself, which serves as the main trailhead for this hike.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de Gartxot suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Ruta de Gartxot is generally considered a moderate hike. While some sections are gentle, it includes ascending paths, narrow and somewhat steep descents, and an elevation gain of around 477 meters. Hikers should have good physical condition and steady footing. It's not ideal for absolute beginners without some prior hiking experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse. It starts briefly on a road before entering the dense Irati Forest with ascending paths through beech and oak trees. You'll encounter waterfalls, forest tracks, and open areas offering panoramic views. The descent includes narrow, steep paths through pine forests, transitioning to gentler stretches across green meadows. Be aware that during periods of high water, a fixed rope might be needed to cross the Moive Ravine.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Ruta de Gartxot?
faqs.answer: This route offers several captivating sights. You'll pass by the stunning La Krutxea Viewpoint, offering spectacular views of the Navarro-Aragonese Pyrenees. Another natural highlight is the beautiful Ripalda Waterfall. The trail's namesake, the Elkorreta-Gartxot Cave, is also a key feature, steeped in local legend.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Ruta de Gartxot is particularly vibrant during autumn when the beech and oak forests display spectacular colors. Spring also offers lush greenery and flowing waterfalls. While possible in other seasons, always check weather conditions, especially for winter hiking, as some sections might be challenging with snow or ice.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de Gartxot dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the Ruta de Gartxot. However, as it passes through the Irati Forest, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike or the Irati Forest?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Ruta de Gartxot itself. The trail is located within the Irati Forest, which is generally accessible without a fee for hiking. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit, especially during peak seasons or specific events.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta de Gartxot largely follows the 'Gartxoten Bidea' trail (100% coverage). It also briefly intersects with sections of other local paths such as 'Elkorreta. Gartxoten kobazuloko bidea' and 'Xinto bidea'.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: As the trail winds through the dense Irati Forest, one of Europe's largest beech forests, you might spot various forest birds, deer, or wild boar, especially during quieter times of the day. Keep an eye out for diverse flora, particularly during spring and autumn.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the route?
faqs.answer: While the trail features several waterfalls and streams like the Regata de Petranberroa, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your hike. Natural water sources should not be relied upon for drinking without proper purification.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend sturdy hiking boots, appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, rain gear, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like the komoot app), and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be helpful for the ascents and descents.
